ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'black tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301103); no specific Sun Moon Lake cultivar entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers black tea as a category; no Sun Moon Lake-specific entry exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 represents black tea as a substance class; no cultivar-specific code for Sun Moon Lake exists.

main_ingredients: Sun Moon Lake black tea leaves (Camellia sinensis var. assamica, Taiwan cultivar TRES No. 18 or No. 8)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 ml (one cup) of brewed Sun Moon Lake black tea, equivalent to roughly 240 g, typically served hot or over ice without added milk or sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 1104262)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Sun Moon Lake black tea is a premium Taiwanese Camellia sinensis var. assamica cultivar. No dedicated entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific tea. USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea (FDC ID 1104262) is used as the closest defensible analogue. Nutrient values reflect plain brewed black tea without milk or sugar. The beverage is essentially calorie-free; all core macros are naturally zero or trace.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson et al.) — plain brewed black tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrate; GI convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea contains approximately 0.3 g carbohydrate per 100 g, of which negligible amounts are glycemic. The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a specific GI value for plain black tea because its carbohydrate content is below the threshold for meaningful GI testing. By established convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages (water, plain tea, black coffee), glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value), gi_category is 'low', and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr, 2021).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); contains flavonoids that may support vascular function
hypertension.guidance: Plain Sun Moon Lake black tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content (approximately 7 mg per 240 ml serving); regular consumption of unsweetened black tea has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in meta-analyses, though patients should avoid adding high-sodium additives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and glycemic load; polyphenols may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Sun Moon Lake black tea has a glycemic load of effectively zero per serving and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; theaflavins and thearubigins in black tea have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in observational studies, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided no sugar or high-GI sweeteners are added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and cholesterol; rich in flavonoid antioxidants (theaflavins, thearubigins)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Sun Moon Lake black tea contains no saturated fat or dietary cholesterol and provides flavonoid antioxidants that have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function; it is a heart-healthy beverage choice when consumed without added sugar, cream, or full-fat milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sun Moon Lake Black Tea (日月潭紅茶; Rì Yuè Tán Hóng Chá) is a premium Taiwanese black tea produced from Camellia sinensis var. assamica cultivars grown in the Sun Moon Lake region of Nantou County, Taiwan, at elevations of approximately 750 metres above sea level. The most celebrated cultivars are TRES No. 18 (Ruby Red / Hong Yu) and TRES No. 8 (Assam), developed by the Taiwan Tea Research and Extension Station. The tea is fully oxidised, producing a deep amber-to-mahogany liquor with a distinctive malty, honey-like aroma, mild natural sweetness, and a smooth, low-astringency finish that sets it apart from Indian or Sri Lankan Assam teas. It is typically brewed at 90–95°C for 3–5 minutes and served hot or over ice, without milk or sugar in traditional Taiwanese style, though it is also popular as a base for milk tea. As a plain brewed beverage, Sun Moon Lake black tea is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrate, and has a glycemic load of zero, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is naturally rich in polyphenolic antioxidants including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with anti-inflammatory, cardioprotective, and insulin-sensitising effects in peer-reviewed literature. The tea is vegan, gluten-free, halal, and allergen-free in its plain brewed form. In Singapore, it is served at specialty tea cafes and bubble tea establishments such as Happy Cup, often as a premium single-origin tea option.
common_user_queries: What is Sun Moon Lake black tea?
Is Sun Moon Lake black tea good for diabetics?
How many calories in Sun Moon Lake black tea?
What does Sun Moon Lake black tea taste like?
Is Sun Moon Lake black tea the same as Ruby Red tea?
Is Sun Moon Lake black tea suitable for hypertension?
Where is Sun Moon Lake black tea from?
What cultivar is used in Sun Moon Lake black tea?
Is Sun Moon Lake black tea caffeinated?

confidence_reason: No dedicated nutrient entry for Sun Moon Lake black tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed black tea (FDC ID 1104262), which is the closest defensible approved-source analogue. The adaptation is highly defensible because plain brewed black tea of any cultivar has essentially the same macro profile (near-zero calories, protein, fat, and carbohydrate). GI is set by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact cultivar-specific approved-source entry was found.
notes: 1. Core macros: protein_g is naturally zero as plain brewed black tea contains no protein by nature. fat_g is naturally zero as plain brewed black tea contains no fat by nature. carbohydrate_g (0.3 g per 100 g) reflects trace soluble compounds extracted during brewing; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 1104262. energy_kcal (1 kcal per 100 g) reflects the trace carbohydrate contribution; confirmed zero-calorie in practical terms. sugar_g is naturally zero (confirmed per USDA FDC ID 1104262). dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a brewed liquid tea. 2. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 per 240 ml serving. 3. Sentinel values (-1): No sentinel values (-1) are used in this record; all micronutrient fields are populated with values from USDA FDC ID 1104262 or set to 0.0 where the source reports zero or not detected. 4. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301103, FoodEx2 A00LC, and SNOMED CT 227313005 are all closest_parent matches; no cultivar-specific codes exist for Sun Moon Lake black tea in any of these ontologies. 5. The price of SGD 7.40 at Happy Cup - Funan reflects a premium single-origin tea positioning in the Singapore market. 6. Caffeine content is not included in the per_100g nutrient panel as it is not a standard field in the RESULT template, but plain brewed black tea typically contains 20–60 mg caffeine per 240 ml serving.
